headline: Russian missile strikes hit Kyiv, Kharkiv as Ukraine secures Gripen fighter jets
subheadline: Russian forces launched multiple missile and drone attacks targeting Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Kirovohrad, with rockets moving from Sumy and Bryansk toward central Ukraine.
lede: Russian forces conducted extensive missile and drone strikes across Ukraine, targeting Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Kirovohrad, with rockets advancing from Sumy and Bryansk toward central areas. Ukrainian officials reported power outages in six regions due to the attacks, while the Ombudsman criticized illegal mobilization practices by recruitment centers. In the Middle East, Israeli forces eliminated four Hamas militants and destroyed rocket launch sites in Gaza’s northern area, and demolished homes in the southern Lebanese town of Beit Yahoun. Lebanese military cleared war remnants in Kfar Ruman industrial area, while Iranian officials warned of retaliation and claimed frozen assets were expedited, denying IAEA access claims.

sections.title: Russian missile strikes cripple Ukrainian infrastructure
sections.body: Russian forces launched multiple missile and drone attacks targeting Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Kirovohrad, with rockets moving from Sumy and Bryansk toward central Ukraine. Ukrainian officials reported power outages across six regions due to the strikes, while the Ombudsman highlighted illegal mobilization practices by recruitment centers and called for reforms. Ukraine signed an agreement to acquire 16 Gripen E fighter jets, with deliveries expected by 2029 and immediate C/D model transfers slated for 2027. Russian officials accused Ukraine of escalating tensions by glorifying UPA figures and criticized Western support for prolonging the conflict.

sections.title: Israeli strikes eliminate Hamas militants, demolish Lebanese homes
sections.body: Israeli military eliminated four Hamas militants and destroyed rocket launch sites in Gaza’s northern area, according to @New_security8200/202176. Israeli forces also demolished homes in the southern Lebanese town of Beit Yahoun, per @presstv/196435. The Lebanese army planned to clear war remnants in Kfar Ruman industrial area, as reported by @redlinkleb/29331. Iranian officials warned of strong retaliation against threats, emphasized support for armed forces, and claimed frozen assets were expedited while denying IAEA access claims, per @naya_foriraq/80512 and @presstv/196432.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The concurrent escalation of Russian missile strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure and Israeli operations in Gaza and Lebanon indicates a broader pattern of adversaries exploiting regional tensions to advance strategic objectives. Iranian threats of retaliation and the denial of IAEA access suggest a defiant stance in nuclear negotiations, while Ukraine’s long‑term Gripen acquisition highlights a shift toward modernizing its air force despite immediate tactical deficits.
